Business value item: Communication patterns — cadence clarity and responsiveness in group private mentor and customer channels (with aggregation and privacy rules)
Stakeholder and PM-style signals — consistency updating user stories acceptance criteria and backlog items
Reliability and follow-through — on-time task completion (sprint goals checklist or done states against dates)
Team collaboration — participation in shared artifacts (comments reviews shared links meeting attendance where tracked)
Professional conduct proxies — quality of support requests structured reporting handling of blockers where inferable from workflow
Code and repo signals — structure of contributions over time (PR size frequency branch hygiene review participation tests or lint where available from GitHub)
Architecture and maintainability — modularity layering dependencies duplication (static analysis or assisted review on repo snapshots)
Delivery discipline — mapping commits and PRs to tasks user stories and sprints (traceability)
Design–implementation alignment — Figma or linked design artifacts vs shipped code (for UI or design-heavy roles)
Tooling breadth — stack exposure from repos CI and project types (languages frameworks)
Curated candidate profile — verified identity link to platform activity plus optional highlights (projects roles time ranges)
GitHub portfolio view — read-only presentation of linked repos (recency languages sample scope) without full GitHub fluency
Design portfolio view — Figma or other links as design artifacts with project context
Narrative arc per project — requirements design tasks code demo (user stories links milestones)
Artifacts bundle — presentations demos documents schema links attached to boards
Comparable scorecards — normalized metrics across candidates (soft and hard dimensions)
Role fit templates — weighting soft vs hard signals by job family (backend PM full-stack etc.)
Trends over time — improvement across projects and sprints (growth vs one-shot)
Benchmarking — cohort or anonymized banding where policy allows
Employer workflow — shortlists notes share links audit of views (governance)
Provenance — activity tied to real platform project work not only self-reported GitHub
Consent and scope — what employers may see (redacted chats repo scope time windows)
Fairness and transparency — which signals feed which scores (explainability for candidates and employers)
Employers — faster evidence-based hiring with less résumé noise and more behavioral and technical proof
Candidates — portable proof of skills from real projects not only certificates
Platform — data advantage from integrated workflow (chat tasks repos design timelines) that point tools alone cannot replicate
